warnings: ref_HA: 1 imaginary mode(s) — not a true minimum; pKa is approximate.
ref_A_minus: 1 imaginary mode(s) — not a true minimum; pKa is approximate.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose: Benzoic-acid aqueous acidity (pKa) — computed with the semi-empirical tight-binding method GFN2-xTB (xtb v⟨current⟩), reference-mode protocol in implicit water (ALPB continuum, ε ≈ 78.4) at 298.15 K and 1 atm. The four species (benzoic acid, benzoate, acetic acid, acetate) were each xtb-optimized and frequency-analysed at their input geometries (single-conformer treatment; no Boltzmann averaging). Reference anchoring used the experimental pKa of acetic acid (4.76) supplied via --pka-ref. Charges: HA 0, A⁻ −1, reference HA 0, reference A⁻ −1; all singlet multiplicities. SCF cycles and vibrational analyses converged; soft (< 50 i cm⁻¹) imaginary modes in the reference pair were treated as floppy rotors, adding uncertainty. Headline result (typical xtb reference accuracy ± ≈2 pKa units): pKa = 4.6616407 (dimensionless). Live engine log was written during execution to `pka_<timestamp>.out`; full JSON result stored at the path shown in that log.
